methods.description: Procedure for collecting a gravity core from the seabed and subsampling it for subsequent analysis.

steps.description: A 40 m long gravity core, core ZK2 (23 • 30 38.44" N, 117 • 30 55.94" E), was collected from the coast of the Dongshan Island, Fujian, in June 2015. The core is located in the northwest of the Taiwan Shoal, with a water depth of 37.5 m and a column length of 40 m.
steps.searchable_summary: collect a gravity core from specified location and depth

steps.description: In this analysis, samples were collected at an interval of 2 m, and a total of 20 samples were collected.
steps.searchable_summary: collect subsamples from the core at regular intervals

methods.description: Methodology for processing sediment samples to extract, identify, and count pollen grains, fern spores, freshwater phytoplanktonic remains, and dinoflagellate cysts.

steps.description: All pollen samples were dried and weighed.
steps.searchable_summary: dry and weigh sediment samples

steps.description: Then, an exotic Lycopodium-spore tablet as a maker containing 27,637 ± 593 grains was added to each sample in order to permit the estimation of the pollen concentration before preparation, followed by an alternative treatment with HF (40%) and HCl (10%).
steps.searchable_summary: add Lycopodium marker and treat samples with HF and HCl

steps.description: For sandy samples, they are first elutriated, and suspended solids are separated, precipitated, and then chemically treated.
steps.searchable_summary: elutriate sandy samples, separate and treat suspended solids

steps.description: Subsequently, the residue of the sample is cleaned and placed in an ultrasonic oscillator.
steps.searchable_summary: clean sample residue and apply ultrasonic oscillation

steps.description: The remaining impurities are removed by using a 7 µm mesh sieve, and finally, pollen grains are deposited.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve sample residue using a 7 µm mesh

steps.description: The pollen identification process was performed under a 400× Leica optical microscope, and each sample counted to at least 200 grains.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and count pollen grains under microscope

steps.description: Fern spores, the freshwater phytoplanktonic remains and dinoflagellate cysts were also identified on the pollen slides.
steps.searchable_summary: identify other microfossils on pollen slides

methods.description: Procedure for preparing sediment samples and determining micro-element concentrations using Inductively Coupled Plasma - Mass Spectrometry (ICP-MS).

steps.description: 0.0400 g of the sample was accurately weighed and placed in a Teflon cup, 1.5 mL of HF and 0.5 mL of HNO 3 were added, and the mixture was sealed and digested to at 150°C for 12 h and then cooled.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh sample, add acids (HF, HNO3), seal, digest at 150°C for 12h, and cool

steps.description: The Teflon cup was taken out, the product was weighed and diluted to 40 g (dilution factor was about 1000), and then applied to the micro-element determination by using ICP-MS.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh digested product, dilute to 40g, prepare for ICP-MS analysis

steps.description: ELAN 9000 ICP-MS (PE Company, Groton, CT, USA) was used for the micro-element determination.
steps.searchable_summary: perform micro-element determination using ICP-MS

steps.description: The sensitivity of the ICP-MS instrument was adjusted to 1 ng mL −1 115 In 30,000 cps.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust ICP-MS instrument sensitivity

steps.description: The testing results of GBW07315 (sediment), GBW07316 (sediment), and BHVO-2 (basalt) were found to be consistent with the recommended values. The relative errors of most element determination results were within 5-10%.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze standard reference materials and validate results

methods.description: Procedure for preparing sediment samples and determining trace element concentrations using Inductively Coupled Plasma - Optical Emission Spectrometry (ICP-OES).

steps.description: The trace element determination was performed by using the IRIS Intrepid II XSP ICP-OES (Thermo Electron, Waltham, MA, USA), and the steps were the same as those of the trace element determination.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare sediment samples using the same digestion and dilution steps as for ICP-MS

steps.description: The trace element determination was performed by using the IRIS Intrepid II XSP ICP-OES (Thermo Electron, Waltham, MA, USA)...
steps.searchable_summary: perform trace element determination using ICP-OES

steps.description: The testing results of Al 2 O 3 , CaO, MnO, TiO 2 , GBW07315 (sediment), GBW07316 (sediment), and BHVO-2 were identical to those of the recommended values. The relative errors of the testing results were smaller than 2%.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze standard reference materials and validate results
inputs: standard reference materials (Al2O3, CaO, MnO, TiO2, GBW07315, GBW07316, BHVO-2)

methods.description: Method for determining the age of sediment layers using Accelerator Mass Spectrometry (AMS) 14C dating and calibrating the results to calendar years.

steps.description: Five accelerator mass spectrometry (AMS) 14 C data were measured by using the core ZK2. The age-dating materials were benthic foraminifera and charcoal (surface). Samples were sent to Beta Analytic, Miami, FL, USA, for completing the test.
steps.searchable_summary: select samples (foraminifera, charcoal) and send for AMS 14C dating

steps.description: All 14 C ages were corrected into the calendar years by using the OxCal 4.4 software (available online: https://c14.arch.ox.ac.uk/oxcal/OxCal.html (accessed on 15 October 2021)) with 95.4% probability (High Probability Density Range Method (HPD): MARINE 20) ( Table 1).
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate 14C ages to calendar years using OxCal software and specified parameters

steps.description: The global oceanic carbon reservoir deviation (∆R) was 113 ± 37 an according to the data on the northwest coast of Taiwan [30].
steps.searchable_summary: apply regional delta R correction during calibration

steps.description: The 14 C age at layer 36.5 m was >43,500 a BP, out of the AMS 14 C dating range, so the age-dating result at layer 36.5 m was excluded.
steps.searchable_summary: exclude age results outside the valid dating range

methods.method_name: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) of Elemental Data
methods.description: Application of Principal Component Analysis to major and trace element content data from the sediment core to identify key environmental factors and interpret their implications.

steps.description: In this study, Past, a free software [31], is used to conduct Principal component analysis (PCA) on the contents of major and trace elements in core ZK2, in order to determine the ecological environmental implication of the principal component axis and provide a quantitative/semi-quantitative study of environmental indicators.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Principal Component Analysis (PCA) on major and trace element data

steps.description: Principal component analysis was carried out in order to clarify the environmental implications of major and trace elements in core ZK2, and the scores of each element on the PCA axes (axis 1 and axis 2) are shown in Figure 5.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze element scores on PCA axes

steps.description: The principal component loading diagram ( Figure 5) shows the relationship between different elements, where each vector axis represents each element, and the horizontal and vertical axes (axis 1 and 2) represent the most important principal components.
steps.searchable_summary: generate and interpret PCA loading diagram

steps.description: The cumulative contribution rates of the first and second principal components to the total variance of all elements accounted for 87.36%, with 69.44% of axis 1 and 17.91% of axis 2, respectively. The contribution rates of other principal components are relatively low. This shows that the first and second principal components can explain most of the environment information contained in major and trace elements.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and report cumulative variance explained by principal components

steps.description: In the scatter diagram of axis 1 against axis 2, axis 1 is positively correlated with Al 2 O 3 , TiO 2 , MnO, Cu, Rb, Ni, V, and Ba, but negatively correlated to SiO 2 ( Figure 5). ... Therefore, axis 1 may be associated with the input of terrigenous materials or primary productivity and authigenic deposition. Axis 2 is positively correlated to CaO and Sr ( Figure 5), which are closely related to the action of marine organisms [42,44]. ... Therefore, it was concluded that axis 2 could be represented by marine biogenic matters.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret environmental implications of principal components based on element loadings and known geochemistry
